data.title: Val-d'Or Mining enters into mineral claim purchase agreement
data.description:
• Val-d'Or Mining (TSXV:VZZ:CA) on Thursday said it has entered into a mineral claim purchase agreement with an arms' length party, dated October 8, 2025, pursuant to which the company purchased a 100% interest in three mining claims in the Larder Lake Mining Division in Ontario.
  • In consideration of the purchase, the company shall pay to the vendor $5,000 in cash and shall issue to the vendor 100,000 common shares of the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Runway Growth Finance to acquire SWK
data.description:
• Runway Growth Finance (NASDAQ:RWAY) announced on Thursday that it has entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire SWK (NASDAQ:SWKH), a life science focused finance company that provides minimally dilutive financing to small- and mid-sized commercial-stage healthcare companies.
  • The transaction will be a net asset value (NAV) merger and will have an estimated purchase price of approximately $220 million, based on SWK’s June 30, 2025, reported financials and including estimated SWK transaction expenses.
  • The transaction includes a fixed stock component expected to total $75.5 million in Runway Growth shares valued at closing NAV per share and approximately $145 million in cash.
  • The cash payment will be based on SWK’s final NAV, which will be struck 48 hours prior to closing and reflective of SWK’s accumulated retained earnings between June 30, 2025, and close.
  • Additionally, Runway Growth Capital, in its capacity as Runway Growth’s external investment adviser, will be contributing $9 million in cash for distribution as consideration to the stockholders of SWK separate from and in addition to the consideration described in the first sentence of this paragraph.
  • SWK’s portfolio includes 22 companies, with an approximate fair value of $242 million based on Runway Growth’s estimates conducted as of August 15, 2025.
  • The transaction is expected to close in late 2025 or the first quarter of 2026, pending SWK shareholder and regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions.
  • SWKH closed -0.34% at $14.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: LandBridge sells solar project to energy infrastructure developer
data.description:
• LandBridge Company (NYSE:LB) on Thursday said it has finalized the sale of a solar project to a publicly-traded energy infrastructure developer.
  • In connection with the transaction, LandBridge received an upfront cash payment and the right to receive contingent future cash payments based on the achievement of certain developmental milestones.
  • The solar project is a 3,000-acre photovoltaic solar energy generation project in Reeves County, Texas with a proposed generation capacity of up to 250 MW.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Toogood Gold announces C$2 million private placement
data.description:
• Toogood Gold (TSXV:TGC:CA)  announced on Thursday a non-brokered private placement to raise gross proceeds of up to C$2 million through the issuance of 6.67 million units at C$0.30 per unit.
  • Each unit will comprise one common share and one half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will be exercisable at a price of C$0.45 into one common share for a period of two years from the date of issuance.
  • Toogood intends to use the proceeds of the offering for working capital and ongoing and future exploration programs at the Company's flagship Toogood Gold Project, located on New World Island, Newfoundland.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about October 22, 2025.
  • The company may, in its sole discretion, exercise an over-allotment option pursuant to which it may increase the size of the offering to up to 10 million units for aggregate proceeds of up to C$3 million.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Linamar to acquire Aludyne’s North American assets for $300 million
data.description:
• Linamar (TSX:LNR:CA) announced on Thursday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire select assets of Aludyne’s North American operations for $300 million, expanding the company's manufacturing footprint across North America, notably in the United States.
  • Linamar will fund the acquisition using available liquidity under its existing credit facilities and cash on hand.
  • On the transaction, National Bank Capital Markets acted as the financial advisor to Linamar.
  • LNR:CA closed -1.62% at $71.86.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Former British Prime Minister Sunak to join Microsoft, Anthropic as adviser
data.description:
Former British Prime Minister Rishi Sunak said on Thursday that he will be joining Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) as a senior adviser.

“Microsoft has driven productivity improvements for decades and Anthropic is one of the most exciting AI frontier labs,” Sunak said in a post on LinkedIn. “I have long believed that technology will transform our world and play a key part in determining our future. I am excited to help these two companies, as they address the big strategic questions about how to make tech work for our economies, our security and our society.”

“We stand on the edge of a technological revolution whose impacts will be as profound as those of the industrial revolution: and felt more quickly,” Sunak continued. “In my role as a senior adviser, I want to help these companies ensure that this shift delivers the improvements in all of our lives that it can.”

Sunak, who was Britain's Prime Minister from October 2022 until July 2024, said any proceeds from the roles will be donated in full to The Richmond Project, the charity he and his wife founded. 

Prior to his career in politics, Sunak worked for Goldman Sachs as an analyst and hedge fund The Children's Investment Fund Management. He also started hedge fund Theleme Partners after leaving TCI in 2009.

data.title: Elong Power receives Nasdaq notification of non-compliance
data.description:
• Elong Power Holding Limited (NASDAQ:ELPW) on Thursday said that it has received a notification from Nasdaq regarding non-compliance with several listing rules.
  • The company’s ordinary shares have traded below the minimum closing bid price of $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days, in violation of Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450[a][1].
  • Additionally, the company’s Market Value of Listed Securities (MVLS) has fallen below the required $50 million under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450[b][2][A], and its Market Value of Publicly Held Shares (MVPHS) is below the minimum $15 million as per Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450[b][2][C].
  • The notification does not affect the company's listing or trading on Nasdaq at this time.
  • Elong Power has until April 1, 2026, to regain compliance with the bid price, MVLS, and MVPHS requirements.
  • The company intends to monitor these metrics and take appropriate actions to meet Nasdaq’s continued listing standards.
  • ELPW -1.00% after hours to $0.4222.
  • Source: Press Release
